mechanism_narrative: Declines in the federal funds rate reduce corporate borrowing costs and compress equity discount rates, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next month.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns show no statistical significance (p > 0.05) in a linear regression against FEDFUNDS changes over a 5-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly unemployment rate increase exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions that immediately trigger risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 5-day returns in > 55% of instances where UNRATE rises > 0.2pp month-over-month over a 3-year sample.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in real personal spending and housing starts confirms broad-based consumer demand recovery, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: Joint positive growth in RSAFS and HOUST fails to produce a positive rank IC (> 0.02) for 60-day forward returns in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Declines in the index of industrial production during a monetary tightening cycle indicate capacity constraints and slowing output, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: INDPRO declines during tightening cycles yield positive 20-day forward returns in > 60% of cases, or the mechanism shows negative predictive power in a regime-filtered backtest.

mechanism_narrative: A monthly CPIAUCSL decline exceeding 0.3% signals deflationary pressure that forces central banks to cut rates, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns within one week.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are negative in > 50% of months where CPIAUCSL drops > 0.3%, or the signal loses statistical significance (p > 0.10) in a 4-year rolling test.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls increases aggregate income and corporate revenue expectations, driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next quarter.
failure_condition: PAYEMS growth shows a negative or zero rank IC for 60-day forward returns across all 3-year rolling windows in the sample period.

mechanism_narrative: Divergence where real GDP growth decelerates while real personal spending accelerates indicates unsustainable consumption borrowing, triggering negative S&P 500 returns over the next month.
failure_condition: The GDPC1-RSAFS divergence metric fails to predict negative 20-day returns, with a positive rank IC > 0.04 in out-of-sample validation.

mechanism_narrative: Accelerating industrial production during a post-recession recovery phase confirms supply-side resilience and capacity utilization, generating positive S&P 500 returns within one week.
failure_condition: INDPRO acceleration during recovery phases yields negative 5-day forward returns in > 65% of cases, or the signal shows no predictive power in a regime-filtered walk-forward test.
